name: Frankfurt
flavor_text: Europe's financial capital and Germany's most international city — 30% of residents are foreign-born. Frankfurt is both the engine of European banking and a city wrestling with what cosmopolitanism costs. The ECB sets rates here while refugees sleep in shipping containers nearby.

advisors.name: Klaus Metzger
advisors.agenda: Leverage Frankfurt's position as the EU's financial capital to fund ambitious city modernization.

briefing: Post-Brexit relocation means 50 more financial institutions. The tax base has grown.
But housing costs have followed. The city is becoming unaffordable.
EU funding for smart city infrastructure is available. We need to apply.
disagreement: That spending isn't aligned with EU state aid rules.
We'd be borrowing against a growth projection that hasn't materialized.
roast: We sit on Europe's financial center and run deficits. Remarkable.
The money flows through Frankfurt. Almost none stays.
dialogue.betrayal: I've accepted an advisory position with Deutsche Bank. The city's finances aren't my problem anymore.

advisors.name: Yelena Brandt
advisors.agenda: Secure public-private partnerships to fund social housing without raising property taxes.

briefing: Municipal bonds are cheap now. We should borrow 2 billion for infrastructure.
The real estate lobby is blocking our social housing targets – they want luxury towers.
If we don't spend the EU recovery funds by next year, Brussels takes them back.
disagreement: You're mortgaging the next generation's tax revenue for projects we can't maintain.
Public-private partnerships are just privatization by another name.
roast: You manage the city's money like a gambler on a hot streak.
I've seen more fiscal discipline in a Monopoly game.
dialogue.betrayal: I've accepted a position with the European Central Bank. Your budget deficit is now their problem.

advisors.name: Kommissar Britta Hoffmann
advisors.agenda: Modernize policing through community-based approaches while managing far-right and organized crime threats.

briefing: Far-right incidents are up 18% this year. We need resources.
The Bahnhofsviertel remains a flashpoint. Drug markets, violence, homelessness intersect.
Community policing in Gallus is working. We should expand it.
disagreement: You can't cut police resources while crime is rising.
Surveillance law reform sounds good in theory. Practically it hamstrings us.
roast: My officers are stretched across too many crises at once.
We manage complex issues with simple mandates.
dialogue.betrayal: I've been talking to opposition politicians about your leadership. I support their candidate.

advisors.name: Polizeirat Jürgen Weber
advisors.agenda: Establish a centralized real-time surveillance hub to predict and prevent street violence.

briefing: The new AI-based camera system in the Hauptbahnhof can predict crowd violence before it erupts.
Organized crime clans control half the drug trade. We need RICO-style asset forfeiture.
Cyber threats to city infrastructure are rising – the water system was probed twice last month.
disagreement: You're trading civil liberties for a false sense of security.
Predictive policing targets neighborhoods, not criminals. That's how riots start.
roast: Your 'security strategy' is more cameras and more overtime. Zero innovation.
I've seen better crisis management in a burning dumpster.
dialogue.betrayal: I've shared our surveillance playbook with a private security firm. They pay better and don't second-guess my methods.

advisors.name: Dr. Ingrid Schneider
advisors.agenda: Build affordable housing without destroying the architectural character Frankfurt has rebuilt since 1945.

briefing: Frankfurt rents have doubled in a decade. Median earners can't afford the city center.
The new Dom-Römer quarter is beautiful and full of tourists. Not housing.
Density is the answer. The political will is the problem.
disagreement: Vertical development in historic zones destroys the identity we spent decades rebuilding.
You're solving for investors, not residents.
roast: We plan, the market builds what it wants. My plans are decorative.
Frankfurt looks better from the air than at street level.
dialogue.betrayal: I've issued a public statement against your housing development plan. It's wrong.

advisors.name: Tobias Voss
advisors.agenda: Transform vacant lots and underused rail corridors into climate-resilient public parks and green roofs.

briefing: Summer temperatures in the city center are 8 degrees higher than the suburbs. That kills people.
We can green 200 rooftops and create 15 new pocket parks with existing budget.
The Main riverfront needs flood protection and public access – not another corporate plaza.
disagreement: You're prioritizing trees over housing. People need shelter first.
Green infrastructure doesn't solve the affordability crisis. It's a distraction.
roast: Your climate plan is a single recycling bin and a press release.
I've seen more environmental commitment from a coal mine.
dialogue.betrayal: I've joined an environmental NGO that's suing the city for violating EU air quality laws. See you in court.

advisors.name: Martin Bauer
advisors.agenda: Complete Frankfurt's transition to zero-emission transport on an EU-aligned timeline.

briefing: The U-Bahn network needs 2 billion in modernization. EU co-funding is available.
Car usage is still 40% of commutes. That needs to fall to 20%.
Bike infrastructure in the suburbs is a political third rail.
disagreement: Pausing the bus electrification sets us back on EU commitments.
That road expansion contradicts every climate promise made in the last five years.
roast: Frankfurt has excellent transit. We still can't convince people to use it.
We run trains on time while the climate burns.
dialogue.betrayal: I've started consulting for a private mobility firm. Public transit is too slow for change.

advisors.name: Leila Sadeghi
advisors.agenda: Expand Frankfurt's river freight capacity to reduce truck traffic on the A5 and A3 autobahns.

briefing: The Main river port operates at 60% capacity. We can double container handling by 2030.
Last-mile delivery emissions are skyrocketing due to Amazon and DHL. We need inner-city micro-hubs.
The airport cargo hub is at capacity. A rail bypass would relieve it.
disagreement: Expanding river freight means dredging and more diesel barges. That's not green.
You're solving logistics while ignoring commuters. The U-Bahn is falling apart.
roast: Your transport strategy is 'hope traffic disappears.' Brilliant.
I've seen faster cargo movement in a wheelchair line.
dialogue.betrayal: The logistics industry offered me a directorship. Your bureaucracy was killing me anyway.

advisors.name: Pastor Ibrahim Al-Masri
advisors.agenda: Bridge Frankfurt's diverse communities through interfaith dialogue and shared civic engagement.

briefing: Frankfurt has 200 nationalities. That's a strength we haven't fully activated.
Antisemitic incidents in the Nordend are increasing. The Jewish community is alarmed.
Muslim communities feel policed rather than welcomed. That gap needs closing.
disagreement: Integration policy that doesn't respect culture creates resentment, not community.
You can't lecture people into belonging.
roast: The city celebrates diversity in brochures but not in housing or employment.
Frankfurt's multiculturalism is a logo. The substance needs work.
dialogue.betrayal: I've joined a civil society coalition challenging your integration policy publicly.

advisors.name: Rabbi Sarah Goldberg
advisors.agenda: Secure protection for Jewish institutions while fostering intergenerational memory work in schools.

briefing: The Jewish community is shrinking because families feel unsafe walking in kippah.
We need police presence outside the Westend synagogue every Saturday – that costs money.
Holocaust education is mandatory in schools, but teachers lack training. I can fix that.
disagreement: Cutting cultural funding for memorial work is short-sighted and cruel.
You say 'never again' but defund the institutions that teach it.
roast: You host memorial ceremonies and vote against our security budget. Hypocrisy has a seat at your table.
My grandparents survived the camps. Your indifference is just quieter.
dialogue.betrayal: I've gone public with your budget cuts. The Jewish community will remember this during elections.

decisions.title: Refugee Housing Integration Plan
decisions.description: The city has 8,000 asylum seekers in temporary accommodation — containers and repurposed factories. A plan to integrate 2,000 into regular city housing faces NIMBY opposition in Sachsenhausen.

options.label: Mandatory dispersal across all districts
options.description: Fair distribution. Wealthy districts resist.

options.label: Voluntary district agreements with incentives
options.description: Negotiate district by district. Slow but consensual.

options.label: Build new satellite settlement
options.description: New development outside city center. Segregated but functional.

decisions.title: Financial Transaction Tax Proposal
decisions.description: The EU is pushing a financial transaction tax that Frankfurt banks are lobbying hard against, threatening to relocate to Dublin or Zurich. The tax could generate 800M city revenue. Implementing alone before EU-wide adoption drives banks away.

options.label: Lobby Berlin for EU-level adoption only
options.description: Correct approach, politically safe, very slow.

options.label: Implement small local banking levy
options.description: Compromise. Raises 100M, irritates banks without driving them away.

options.label: Oppose the tax, protect the sector
options.description: Banks stay. Public services suffer.

decisions.title: Bahnhofsviertel Drug Policy
decisions.description: Frankfurt pioneered harm reduction in Europe. The Bahnhofsviertel (red-light/drug district) is either a model of pragmatic policy or a public embarrassment — depending on who you ask. A conservative coalition wants enforcement; health advocates want expansion.

options.label: Expand supervised consumption facilities
options.description: Evidence-based. Conservative backlash.

options.label: Status quo maintenance
options.description: Don't rock the boat. Some problems persist.

options.label: Crackdown on open drug use
options.description: Visible enforcement. Displaces rather than solves.

crises.title: Far-Right March Through Sachsenhausen
crises.description: A far-right group has received a legal permit for a march through the historic Jewish quarter on Holocaust remembrance day. Counter-protesters are gathering. Violence is expected.

options.label: Reroute the march, maximum counter-protest buffer
options.description: Legal but protected. Community feels heard.

options.label: Apply for emergency permit revocation
options.description: Legally uncertain. Sends strong signal.

options.label: Allow march, heavy police presence
options.description: Legal compliance. Jewish community furious.

crises.title: ECB Anti-Austerity Blockade
crises.description: 10,000 protesters have blockaded the ECB tower in response to EU interest rate decisions. The financial district is paralyzed. International media coverage is significant.

options.label: Facilitate dialogue with ECB and protesters
options.description: Play mediator. Limited authority, high visibility.

options.label: Clear the blockade
options.description: Restore economic function. Protest anger redirected at city.

options.label: Formally support protesters' demands
options.description: Symbolic solidarity. ECB relations damaged.

crises.title: Stadtwerke Strike
crises.description: Transit workers are striking over pay and conditions. Every U-Bahn and bus line is stopped. Two million daily commuters are affected. Contract negotiations broke down three days ago.

options.label: Meet union demands
options.description: End strike immediately. Budget hit. Sets precedent.

options.label: Mediation with federal arbitration
options.description: External mediator. Strike continues 2 more weeks.

options.label: Essential services injunction
options.description: Force partial service legally. Unions escalate.

crises.title: Major Bank Relocation Threat
crises.description: Deutsche Bank has privately signaled it will move its headquarters to Zurich unless the city resolves a zoning dispute over its planned new campus. 8,000 jobs and 500M in annual tax revenue are at stake.

options.label: Approve the campus as requested
options.description: Bank stays. Historic neighborhood impacted.

options.label: Counter with modified site proposal
options.description: Negotiate location. May or may not work.

options.label: Call their bluff publicly
options.description: Dare them to leave. High risk, high principle.

scandals.title: Affordable Housing Contract Fraud
scandals.description: Affordable housing contracts were awarded to a developer linked to a city official.

scandals.title: Refugee Data Leak
scandals.description: Asylum seeker personal data was found shared with a far-right group.

scandals.title: Private Banker Dinner Revealed
scandals.description: The governor was photographed at a private dinner with bank executives during zoning negotiations.

positive: Finally, governance that reflects our values.
This is the Frankfurt I believe in.
Endlich — the Stadtrat does something worth cheering about.
Von Bockenheim bis Sachsenhausen, people are breathing easier today.
The ECB runs rates. We run the city. And today we ran it well.
This is what strong progressive governance looks like. Bravo.
neutral: Another day in the financial capital.
Progress is slow here. But it happens.
The ECB will do what it wants anyway.
Abwarten. Frankfurt has been through worse and moved on.
Not the boldest move, but the S-Bahn still runs and so does the city.
The Stadtrat compromises. As always. As expected.
Between the Banken and the Bürger, something gets done eventually.
negative: Mieten explodieren. I can't afford this city.
The banks are welcome. The refugees are not. I see how it is.
Frankfurt works for finance. Not for families.
Gallus families are being pushed out while Westend bankers get tax breaks. Unglaublich.
The Mietendeckel debate goes nowhere. My landlord raised rent again. Danke.
Another U-Bahn delay, another excuse. I've been standing at Konstablerwache for 40 minutes.
Wir zahlen Steuern damit Deutsche Bank seinen Bonus schützt. Wir sind doch nicht dumm.
crisis: Frankfurt is resilient. But it's being tested.
The city needs leadership, not management.
When the ECB tower is blockaded and the U-Bahn is striking, something has fundamentally broken.
Bahnhofsviertel is boiling over. This was predictable and predicted. Niemand hat zugehört.
Frankfurt survived the war and rebuilt itself. But this administration may be the bigger test.
Von der Finanzkrise bis heute — wir lösen immer die Symptome, nie das Problem.
scandal: I expect better from this administration.
Europe is watching and we embarrass ourselves.
Private dinners with Deutsche Bank while they demand zoning favors? Das ist Korruption, Punkt.
Refugee data shared with AfD-adjacent networks? Someone must resign. Sofort.
FAZ has the documents. Frankfurter Rundschau has the sources. There's nowhere left to hide.
The city that hosts the ECB can't manage its own housing contracts honestly. Schande.
media: FAZ EXKLUSIV: Stadtrat stimmt über umstrittenen Wohnungsbauplan ab — Sachsenhausen in Aufruhr
Frankfurter Rundschau: Mieten in Bockenheim steigen um 22% in drei Jahren — Verdrängung beschleunigt sich
@FAZ_NET: BREAKING — ECB tower blockade enters day two, financial district access restricted #Frankfurt
Frankfurter Rundschau: AfD Stadtratsfraktion fordert Schließung aller Druckräume im Bahnhofsviertel
hr-fernsehen LIVE: Stadtwerke-Streik legt U-Bahn lahm — 2 Millionen Pendler betroffen
@FR_Redaktion: Exklusiv — Wohnungsbauvertrag an Unternehmen vergeben, das mit Stadtrat verbunden ist
FAZ: Deutsche Bank droht mit Umzug nach Zürich — Stadtplanung unter Druck
Hessenschau: Rechtsextreme Demo durch Sachsenhausen genehmigt — Gegenprotest angekündigt
politician: SPD Stadtratsfraktion: 'Wir brauchen einen echten Mietendeckel — keine Placebos für Vermieter.'
Grüne Frankfurt: 'The ECB sets rates. We set priorities. And our priority is affordable housing.'
AfD Stadtrat: 'Ordnung im Bahnhofsviertel zuerst — danach reden wir über Integration.'
CDU Frankfurt: 'Deutsche Bank muss bleiben. Kompromisse beim Zoning sind dafür akzeptabel.'
Oberbürgermeister-Büro: 'We will not allow Frankfurt to become a city only bankers can afford.'
SPD: 'Der Stadtwerke-Streik zeigt — wir haben den öffentlichen Sektor jahrelang vernachlässigt.'
Grüne Stadtrat: 'The supervised consumption rooms in Bahnhofsviertel save lives. The data is clear.'
AfD Pressemitteilung: 'Massenintegration ohne Plan gefährdet Frankfurts Sicherheit und Kultur.'
activist: Mieterforum Frankfurt: 'Jede Familie, die aus Bockenheim verdrängt wird, ist ein Politikversagen.'
AWO Frankfurt: 'We have 8,000 people in containers. Europe's richest city can do better than this.'
Oury Jalloh Initiative Frankfurt: 'Integration policy is meaningless without anti-racism in the police.'
BUND Frankfurt: 'U-Bahn modernization is a climate issue. Fund it now or pay more later.'
Paritätischer Wohlfahrtsverband Hessen: 'Refugees are not a burden. They are our neighbors. Treat them so.'
Interventionistische Linke FFM: 'Wenn Banken Zoning diktieren, ist das keine Demokratie.'
Initiative Schwarzes Frankfurt: 'Policing in Gallus targets communities, not crimes. We document this.'
Frankfurt Habitat: 'The Dom-Römer is beautiful. But beauty doesn't house the Gallus families being evicted.'
street: Alter, die Miete in Nordend ist wieder gestiegen. Ich zieh bald nach Offenbach ob ich will oder nicht.
The S6 to Darmstadt was cancelled again. Third time this week. Ich dreh durch.
Sachsenhausen Äppelwoi mit the boys tonight. Politik kann warten.
Bahnhofsviertel has its issues ja, but it's the only real neighborhood left in this city. Alles andere ist für Banker.
My landlord in Bornheim just sent a Modernisierungsumlage letter. 200 Euro mehr im Monat. Danke, Stadtrat.
Westend? More like Westbank. Kann sich kein normaler Mensch mehr leisten.
The ECB protest is three blocks from my office. My boss sent us home. Erster bezahlter Freiheit seit Jahren, lol.
Gallus used to be working class. Now it's 'up and coming.' Meaning: wir werden vertrieben.

scandal_reactions.minor: The situation has been logged. A report will be prepared.
scandal_reactions.moderate: Oversight committee has been convened. This is unacceptable.
scandal_reactions.major: Legal counsel has been engaged. The governor must answer to parliament.
scandal_reactions.career_ending: This is incompatible with public office. Exit the position immediately.

romance_exposure.flavour: It is a distraction from governance. The committee is not pleased.

city_personality.slang_reaction: Das ist doch ein absoluter Witz, so ein Chaos!
